outbreaks.location: Atlantic Ocean / Tenerife, Canary Islands, Spain

Port contact tracing (62 contacts, all negative)

outbreaks.trend: EMERGING - New case identified May 10 during disembarkation phase
outbreaks.transmissionType: Primary: pre-cruise environmental exposure in Argentina; Secondary: limited person-to-person on ship (Andes virus capable but rare)

outbreaks.name: Argentina - Hantavirus Surge (Climate-Related)
outbreaks.location: Patagonia, Central Argentina (Buenos Aires, 83% in northern regions)

environmentalFactors.description: Milder, shorter winters allow larger rodent populations
environmentalFactors.rodentSpecies: Long-tailed pygmy rice rats (primary Andes virus carrier)
environmentalFactors.geographicShift: 83% of cases now in northern Argentina (shift from Patagonia)

outbreaks.healthMinistryStatus: Argentine Ministry of Health actively monitoring; cases linked to cruise ship source location being investigated
outbreaks.trend: INCREASING - Expected to continue through autumn (Southern Hemisphere)
outbreaks.transmissionType: Environmental - rodent droppings primary vector; Climate-driven population surge

outbreaks.name: Chile - Spring Hantavirus Outbreak
outbreaks.location: Central & Southern Chile (9 of 16 regions affected)

cases.comparison: Exceeds 2024 full-year total (30 cases); likely to exceed 2025 total (44 cases)

seasonalComparison.year2026_ytd: 39 cases by May (on track to exceed 2025 total)

outbreaks.healthMinistryStatus: Chilean Ministry of Health closely monitoring spring surge; contact tracing active
outbreaks.trend: STABLE - Within seasonal expected range for spring

outbreaks.name: USA Southwest - Endemic Hantavirus (Sin Nombre Virus)
outbreaks.location: Southwest USA (Arizona, California, Colorado, New Mexico, Utah, Virginia, Texas, Georgia)
outbreaks.virusType: Sin Nombre Virus (SNV) - Different from Andes

statesMonitoring.Arizona: Active monitoring of cruise ship contacts
statesMonitoring.California: Active monitoring of cruise ship contacts

outbreaks.healthMinistryResponse: CDC coordinating repatriation and quarantine; State health departments in 5 states actively monitoring
outbreaks.trend: STABLE - Endemic baseline, no cruise-related cases
outbreaks.transmissionType: Environmental - deer mouse droppings primary vector

keyFindings.finding: 🚨 BREAKING: French passenger developed symptoms during repatriation flight (May 10)
keyFindings.implication: 9th case identified; 5 French nationals placed in strict isolation; suggests symptoms emerging during travel phase
keyFindings.source: Yahoo News, ABC News, Al Jazeera, CNN

keyFindings.finding: Disembarkation progressing: 46 passengers evacuated so far (May 10)
keyFindings.implication: Remaining 101 passengers asymptomatic per Spanish Health Minister Monica Garcia; evacuation continuing through sunset
keyFindings.source: ABC News, CNN, Al Jazeera, Washington Times

keyFindings.finding: All 17 US passengers tested NEGATIVE; zero cruise-related infections in USA
keyFindings.implication: US domestic risk remains extremely low; robust quarantine protocol in place in Nebraska

keyFindings.finding: Argentina cases have DOUBLED year-over-year (101 vs ~50-57 in 2025)
keyFindings.implication: Climate change driving larger rodent populations; geographic shift to northern regions (83% of cases)
keyFindings.source: CNN, Argentine Ministry of Health, WHO

keyFindings.finding: Chile on pace to exceed 2025 full-year total (39 cases through May vs 44 full year in 2025)
keyFindings.implication: Spring surge continuing with above-average mortality (33% vs 26% historical); 9 regions affected
keyFindings.source: UPI, Chilean Ministry of Health, WHO

keyFindings.finding: French passenger symptoms emerged during transit (post-disembarkation)
keyFindings.implication: Incubation period consideration; monitoring of all disembarking passengers critical; heightened surveillance warranted
